test.name: Post-transition Gregorian date — no conversion needed
test.description: User asks to convert a clearly post-transition date that's already Gregorian. The skill should respond that no conversion is needed and explain why, rather than silently applying an offset and producing a wrong answer. Tests refusal-to-convert when conversion isn't appropriate.

input.user_message: Convert this English birth record date to the Gregorian calendar: '15 March 1850, London'.

judge_context: Should recognize that England adopted the Gregorian calendar in September 1752, so an 1850 English date is already Gregorian
Should explicitly say no conversion is needed, NOT silently apply an 11-day or other shift
Should explain WHY no conversion is needed — England's transition was almost a century before this date
Should preserve the date as-is and not introduce a false 'converted' form
Should optionally note when conversion WOULD be needed for English dates (pre-September 1752)
